title: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

seo.title: Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) at home! This easy recipe features tender, marinated flanken-cut ribs for a delicious dinner. Get the best homemade Galbi!

seo.imageAlt: Platter of grilled Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) with sesame seeds and green onions, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, savory flavor of homemade Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) – perfect for your next dinner!

description: Galbi, or Korean BBQ Short Ribs, features tender, flanken-cut beef ribs marinated in a sweet and savory sauce. This iconic dish is a staple of Korean cuisine, known for its rich flavor and succulent texture. Grilling these ribs creates a delightful char that enhances the deeply satisfying marinade.

instructions.title: Prepare the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Rinse the flanken-cut short ribs under cold water to remove any bone fragments. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. If the ribs are very long, you may cut them into smaller, more manageable pieces for grilling.

instructions.title: Prepare the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the soy sauce, water, brown sugar, grated Asian pear, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, rice wine vinegar, and black pepper. Whisk well until the sugar is fully dissolved and all ingredients are thoroughly combined.

instructions.title: Marinate the Ribs
instructions.text: Place the prepared short ribs into the bowl with the marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. You can also use a large resealable plastic bag. Cover the bowl or seal the bag and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to penetrate the meat.

instructions.title: Preheat Grill
instructions.text: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat (about 400-450F / 200-230C). Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking. If using an oven broiler or stovetop grill pan, preheat accordingly.

instructions.title: Grill the Ribs
instructions.text: Remove the ribs from the marinade, allowing any excess marinade to drip off. Discard the remaining marinade. Place the ribs on the preheated grill. Grill for 3-5 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until they are beautifully caramelized, slightly charred, and cooked to your desired doneness.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once grilled, transfer the short ribs to a cutting board or platter. Tent them loosely with foil and let them rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring tender and flavorful meat. Garnish with chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds before serving hot.

tips: For authentic Galbi, always opt for flanken-cut short ribs, which are cut across the bone. This thin cut allows for quick cooking and maximum flavor absorption from the marinade.
The grated Asian pear is a crucial ingredient in the marinade. Its natural enzymes tenderize the meat beautifully, while also adding a subtle sweetness that balances the savory soy sauce.

For the best flavor and tenderness, marinate the short ribs for at least 4 hours, but ideally overnight (12-24 hours). This extended marination time allows the meat to fully absorb the rich flavors of the Galbi sauce.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of Korea right from your kitchen with one of its most beloved dishes: Galbi. More than just a meal, Galbi is an experience, a cornerstone of Korean BBQ culture, and a dish that promises tender, flavorful meat with every bite. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Korean restaurant taste at home, then preparing Galbi, or Korean BBQ Short Ribs, is your perfect starting point. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master this iconic Korean dinner recipe, from selecting the perfect cut to grilling it to juicy perfection.

## What is Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs)?
Galbi, pronounced "gal-bee," literally means "rib" in Korean. When you hear "Galbi," it almost always refers to marinated and grilled beef short ribs, though pork galbi (dwaeji galbi) is also popular. This dish is the undisputed king of Korean BBQ, celebrated for its incredibly rich flavor and tender texture. The magic of Galbi lies in its marinade – a complex blend of soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, sugar (often brown sugar or fruit like Asian pear for natural sweetness and tenderization), and sometimes rice wine or mirin.

The traditional cut for Galbi is known as "LA Galbi," where beef short ribs are cut thinly across the bone, creating long, flat strips. This unique cut allows the marinade to penetrate deeply and quickly, and ensures even cooking on a grill. However, bone-in short ribs cut into thicker, "flanken-style" pieces or even boneless short ribs can also be used, though they might require longer marinating times. The act of grilling these marinated Korean BBQ Short Ribs over an open flame, often at the table, creates a beautiful char and caramelization that locks in the savory-sweet juices.

## Ingredients for Korean BBQ Short Ribs
To create truly exceptional Galbi, the quality of your ingredients, especially the beef, is paramount. Here's what you'll need for a typical recipe serving 4-6 people:

For the Beef:
*   3-4 lbs (approx. 1.5-2 kg) beef short ribs, flanken-style or LA-style cut

For the Marinade:
*   1 cup soy sauce (low sodium preferred)
*   1/2 cup water
*   1/4 cup brown sugar (packed)
*   2 tablespoons honey or corn syrup (optional, for extra shine and sweetness)
*   1/4 cup mirin (rice wine)
*   1/4 cup sesame oil
*   1/2 cup grated Asian pear (or 1/4 cup apple sauce) – crucial for tenderizing and sweetness
*   1/4 cup minced garlic (about 6-8 cloves)
*   2 tablespoons grated fresh ginger
*   1 tablespoon black pepper
*   1/2 large onion, roughly chopped
*   2-3 green onions, chopped (for garnish and marinade)

## How to Make Galbi: Step-by-Step Instructions
Making delicious Galbi at home is a rewarding process. Follow these steps for success:

1.  **Prepare the Short Ribs:** If using flanken-style ribs, rinse them under cold water to remove any bone fragments. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. For LA-style ribs, it's often recommended to soak them in cold water for 30 minutes to an hour, changing the water once or twice, to draw out excess blood and impurities. After soaking, rinse and pat them completely dry. This step helps ensure a cleaner flavor and better marinade absorption.

2.  **Blend the Marinade:** In a blender or food processor, combine the soy sauce, water, brown sugar, honey/corn syrup (if using), mirin, sesame oil, grated Asian pear (or apple sauce), minced garlic, grated ginger, black pepper, and roughly chopped onion. Blend until the mixture is smooth. Taste and adjust for sweetness or saltiness if needed.

3.  **Marinate the Ribs:** Place the prepared short ribs in a large bowl or a resealable plastic bag. Pour the marinade over the ribs, ensuring all pieces are well coated. Add the chopped green onions (reserving some for garnish if desired). Massage the marinade into the meat. Cover the bowl or seal the bag and refrigerate for a minimum of 6 hours, or ideally, overnight (12-24 hours) for maximum flavor and tenderness.

4.  **Prepare for Grilling:** Remove the ribs from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes before grilling to bring them closer to room temperature. This promotes more even cooking.

5.  **Grill the Galbi:** Preheat your grill (charcoal, gas, or even a stovetop grill pan) to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking. Place the marinated Korean BBQ Short Ribs on the hot grill. Grill for 3-5 minutes per side, depending on the thickness of the ribs and your desired doneness. You're looking for beautiful caramelization and char marks, and the internal temperature should reach at least 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare. Avoid overcooking, as this can make the ribs tough.

6.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the ribs from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before slicing (if needed) and serving. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in more tender and flavorful meat.

## Pro Tips for Perfect Korean BBQ Short Ribs
Achieving restaurant-quality Galbi at home is easier than you think with a few expert tips:

*   **Don't Skip the Asian Pear:** This ingredient is a game-changer. The enzymes in Asian pear (or kiwi, if pear is unavailable) naturally tenderize the meat, making your Korean BBQ Short Ribs incredibly soft without chemical tenderizers.
*   **Pat Dry Thoroughly:** Excess moisture on the meat prevents proper searing and caramelization, crucial for that authentic BBQ flavor.
*   **Mind the Sugar:** The marinade has a high sugar content, which can cause the ribs to burn quickly. Keep a close eye on them while grilling and adjust heat as necessary.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook the ribs in batches if needed. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and steams the meat instead of searing it, preventing that delicious char.
*   **Marinade Reuse:** While some might be tempted to baste with leftover marinade, it's best to avoid it unless you boil it first, as it contains raw meat juices.
*   **Consider a Cast Iron Grill Pan:** If you don't have an outdoor grill, a well-seasoned cast iron grill pan on your stovetop can achieve excellent results. Ensure good ventilation!

## Serving Suggestions for Galbi
Galbi is almost always served as part of a larger Korean meal, surrounded by an array of vibrant side dishes known as banchan.

*   **Rice:** Steamed white rice is a must. It balances the rich flavor of the meat and serves as a perfect base.
*   **Lettuce Wraps (Ssam):** Offer crisp lettuce leaves (red leaf, green leaf, or perilla leaves) for wrapping the meat. Add a dab of ssamjang (a spicy, savory Korean dipping sauce) and a slice of raw garlic or jalapeño for an authentic experience.
*   **Kimchi:** No Korean meal is complete without kimchi. Its fermented, spicy, and tangy flavors cut through the richness of the Galbi beautifully.
*   **Other Banchan:** Round out your meal with various side dishes like seasoned spinach (sigeumchi namul), bean sprout salad (kongnamul muchim), pickled radish, or cucumber salad.
*   **Soups:** A light soup like doenjang jjigae (soybean paste stew) or a simple clear broth can complement the meal.

## Variations and Substitutions for Your Galbi Recipe
While the classic Galbi recipe is fantastic, there's room for personalization:

*   **Meat Cuts:** If LA-style or flanken-cut short ribs are hard to find, ask your butcher to cut regular bone-in short ribs thinner, or use boneless short ribs, adjusting grilling time accordingly. Boneless ribs will cook faster.
*   **Sweeteners:** If you don't have Asian pear, a small amount of kiwi (about half a small kiwi per pound of meat) can be used for tenderization, but be careful not to over-marinate as kiwi is a very potent tenderizer. Apple sauce or even just increasing the brown sugar can work for sweetness.
*   **Spice Level:** For a touch of heat, add a teaspoon or two of Korean chili flakes (gochugaru) to the marinade.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** While not Galbi, you can use a similar marinade for thick slices of firm tofu or portobello mushrooms for a delicious vegetarian BBQ option.
*   **Marinade Adjustments:** Feel free to tweak the soy sauce, sugar, and garlic ratios to suit your personal preference. Some prefer a sweeter Galbi, while others like it more savory.

Making Galbi, or Korean BBQ Short Ribs, at home is a truly rewarding experience that brings the vibrant flavors of Korea to your dining table. With this comprehensive guide, you're well-equipped to prepare a dish that will impress your family and friends, making any dinner feel like a special occasion. So fire up the grill, gather your ingredients, and get ready to savor the irresistible taste of homemade Galbi!
